subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
Midnight on the county line, jar of fire in the back
Daddy's old recipe, ridin' down that gravel track
Headlights cut the holler, moon hung low and mean
Fastest little Chevy that these backroads ever seen

[Chorus]
Run, run, the moon is high
Whiskey in the tank and a prayer up to the sky
Lord I took a wrong turn where the river meets the bend
This moonshine run is where my luck is gonna end

[Verse]
Blue light in the mirror, heart a-poundin' like a drum
Jars went rollin' over when I felt that engine come
Smoke up off the manifold, the curve came way too fast
Now the cornfield's got my bumper and the bottle's breakin' glass

[Bridge]
Mama always told me that the easy money lies
Now I'm sittin' in the ditch with the smoke up in my eyes

[Chorus]
Run, run, the moon is high
Whiskey in the tank and a prayer up to the sky
Lord I took a wrong turn where the river meets the bend
This moonshine run is where my luck is gonna end

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
The iron snake it swallows me beneath the city's bones
The lights they flicker like the candles in the church of stone
My heart's a hammer on the anvil, breath a broken hymn
The walls they lean in closer and the platform's growin' dim

[Chorus]
Oh the dark train rumbles low
And there's nowhere left to go
I'm drownin' in the tunnel where the cold wind learns my name
And the devil's in the wheels of this midnight metal train

[Verse]
The strangers wear their hollow eyes like masks upon the grave
I count the rivets one by one, too frightened to be brave
The doors won't open soon enough, the air is turnin' thin
The rattle of the rails becomes the rattle of my skin

[Bridge]
Hold on, hold on, the station's drawin' near
The daylight's just a promise that I'm too afraid to hear

[Chorus]
Oh the dark train rumbles low
And there's nowhere left to go
I'm drownin' in the tunnel where the cold wind learns my name
And the devil's in the wheels of this midnight metal train

subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three in the mornin', neon on the dash
Thousand miles behind me and the rain comes down so fast
I dial your number slow, baby, just to hear you breathe
The highway's got my body but my heart it never leaves

[Chorus]
So pick up the phone, let me hear your voice
This lonely road don't ever give a man a choice
I'm callin' you at 3am from a world away
Just to tell you that I love you and I'm comin' home someday

[Verse]
The diesel hums a lullaby, the moon's my only friend
I see your face in every mile from here until the end
The coffee's cold, the radio's a whisper in the dark
But your sleepy little hello, girl, it lights up all my sparks

[Bridge]
Kiss the babies for me, tell 'em daddy's on his way
Just a few more sunrise miles and I'll be home to stay

[Chorus]
So pick up the phone, let me hear your voice
This lonely road don't ever give a man a choice
I'm callin' you at 3am from a world away
Just to tell you that I love you and I'm comin' home someday

subject: a factory closing down
lyrics:
[Verse]
The whistle blows its final breath into the silver haze
The windows hold the ghosts of all our golden working days
The smokestacks fade to memory, the gates are chained and still
And the echoes of the assembly line are drifting down the hill

[Chorus]
Fade away, oh fade away
Like the lights of yesterday
We were steel and we were thunder, now we're soft as falling snow
In the factory of our dreaming where the quiet rivers flow

[Verse]
My father gave his hands to it, my mother gave her years
The rust is blooming slowly on the things we held so dear
The shadows dance on empty floors where once the engines sang
And somewhere in the silence still I hear the morning clang

[Bridge]
The town will learn to dream again beneath a paler sun
Though the heartbeat of the iron age is gone now, gone and done

[Chorus]
Fade away, oh fade away
Like the lights of yesterday
We were steel and we were thunder, now we're soft as falling snow
In the factory of our dreaming where the quiet rivers flow

subject: a grandmother's hands
lyrics:
[Verse]
They were soft as Sunday morning and as strong as mountain stone
They kneaded bread at sunrise in a kitchen warm as home
Every line a river, every wrinkle was a road
They carried all our heavy days and never dropped the load

[Chorus]
Grandmother's hands, oh grandmother's hands
They held the whole world gentle like the soft of mended lands
They wiped away the teardrops and they taught us how to pray
Grandmother's hands still hold me though she's been so long away

[Verse]
She quilted up the winters and she rocked the babies slow
She planted every garden that a family needs to grow
The rings upon her fingers caught the gold of evening light
And her touch upon my forehead made the darkness feel less bright

[Bridge]
Now the rocking chair is empty but the love it lingers on
In the hands she passed to all of us before she up and gone

[Chorus]
Grandmother's hands, oh grandmother's hands
They held the whole world gentle like the soft of mended lands
They wiped away the teardrops and they taught us how to pray
Grandmother's hands still hold me though she's been so long away

subject: texting someone you shouldn't
lyrics:
[Verse]
Soft the evening falls on Ipanema's golden sand
My phone is glowing gently in my trembling guilty hand
Your name lights up the darkness like a forbidden little star
I know I shouldn't write you but I wonder where you are

[Chorus]
Oh the words I shouldn't say
Drift like smoke and float away
Da da da, my heart it knows the danger and the sweet
But my fingers keep on typing to the rhythm of the beat

[Verse]
The ocean keeps its secrets and the moon won't say a word
A whispered conversation that the world has never heard
I press the little arrow and I watch the bubble fly
A quiet little sin beneath a soft Brazilian sky

[Bridge]
Delete, delete, but my thumb won't let it go
The samba of temptation moving gentle, moving slow

[Chorus]
Oh the words I shouldn't say
Drift like smoke and float away
Da da da, my heart it knows the danger and the sweet
But my fingers keep on typing to the rhythm of the beat

subject: a chess hustler in the park
lyrics:
[Verse]
Down on the corner where the pigeons take their flight
There's a man behind the chessboard playin' day into the night
Five dollars says you can't beat me, baby, take a seat
His fingers move like lightning and they never taste defeat

[Chorus]
King me, queen me, watch the clock run down
He's the smoothest little hustler in the whole of downtown
Checkmate, sucker, that's another fool he's fleeced
The baddest cat in Washington Square, the king of all the pieces

[Verse]
He talks a velvet river while his bishop slides on through
He's three moves up the avenue before you got a clue
The saxophone of swindle and the rhythm of the rook
He'll take your last few dollars with a sly and easy look

[Bridge]
But every king gets checkmated when the young guns come to play
And somewhere down the road the board will sweep his crown away

[Chorus]
King me, queen me, watch the clock run down
He's the smoothest little hustler in the whole of downtown
Checkmate, sucker, that's another fool he's fleeced
The baddest cat in Washington Square, the king of all the pieces

subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
Headlights bleed across the pines, a slow and rising hum
The jars are singing softly to the beating of a drum
The road unspools like memory beneath a silver moon
And somewhere in the distance we can feel it coming soon

[Chorus]
Rise, rise, the engines roar
We were never meant to make it to the other shore
The night is breaking open and the fire's in the glass
We are falling like the embers through the cold and shattered grass

[Verse]
The river bends to meet us as the tires lose their hold
The quiet turns to thunder and the warm gives way to cold
The stars come down to find us where the metal meets the clay
And every dream we carried gently slips and drifts away

[Bridge]
Let it build, let it swell, let the silence finally break
In the wreckage of the holler for the wrong turn that we make

[Chorus]
Rise, rise, the engines roar
We were never meant to make it to the other shore
The night is breaking open and the fire's in the glass
We are falling like the embers through the cold and shattered grass

subject: a museum guard and a painting
lyrics:
[Verse]
Every morning at the doorway in my faded navy blue
There's a girl inside a gold frame and she's all I ever knew
She's been waiting there two hundred years with paint upon her face
And I'm guarding all her silence in this quiet marble place

[Chorus]
Oh she never says a word
But she's the loudest thing I've heard
I'm just a guard in an empty hall and she's a work of art
But the painted girl in the golden frame, she's got my foolish heart

[Verse]
The tourists come and tourists go, they barely turn their heads
They snap a photo, check the time, and walk on by instead
But I have memorized her eyes, the way the light will fall
And every night at closing time I tell her through the wall

[Bridge]
Maybe I'm just lonely, maybe love is what I lack
But she looks at me like she knows me and I swear she looks right back

[Chorus]
Oh she never says a word
But she's the loudest thing I've heard
I'm just a guard in an empty hall and she's a work of art
But the painted girl in the golden frame, she's got my foolish heart

subject: a museum guard and a painting
lyrics:
[Verse]
Uniform crisp, badge on my chest, walkin' through the hall (yeah)
Marble floors, golden frames, I done seen it all (seen it)
But there's one on the wall got me losin' my mind
Painted girl from the past, she the realest you'll find (ooh)

[Chorus]
She don't move, she don't talk, but she own my soul (own it)
Standin' guard every night and I never let go (never)
Frame so gold, eyes so cold, but she lookin' at me (at me)
Museum got a million but she all that I see (yeah)

[Verse]
Flashlight beam in the dark, just me and her (just us)
Centuries of secrets in a quiet little blur (blur)
They pay me minimum but my heart pay the cost
Fell in love with a masterpiece, now my mind is lost (lost)

[Bridge]
Closing time, lights down low, it's just me again (again)
Tellin' her all my dreams like she my oldest friend (friend)

[Chorus]
She don't move, she don't talk, but she own my soul (own it)
Standin' guard every night and I never let go (never)
Frame so gold, eyes so cold, but she lookin' at me (at me)
Museum got a million but she all that I see (yeah)

subject: a card game that went too far
lyrics:
[Verse]
Smoke in the basement, the deck's been dealt
Greed in the eyes and a hand on the belt
Stakes getting higher with every round
Fists on the table, no mercy found

[Chorus]
ALL IN! No way back!
The table's flipped and the room goes black!
YOU PUSHED TOO FAR, NOW THE DEBT IS PAID!
IN THE GAME WE PLAYED, IN THE GAME WE PLAYED!

[Verse]
Aces and liars, the bluff goes cold
More than your money, you sold your soul
Someone stood up and the chairs hit the floor
What started as a game is a game no more

[Bridge]
No more chips, no more lies
Look the devil in his eyes
Everything you had is gone
The last card's flipped, the night moves on

[Chorus]
ALL IN! No way back!
The table's flipped and the room goes black!
YOU PUSHED TOO FAR, NOW THE DEBT IS PAID!
IN THE GAME WE PLAYED, IN THE GAME WE PLAYED!

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
The walls are breathing slowly in the underground
A thousand distant heartbeats and a fading sound
The air grows thin and silver as the tunnel sighs
And I am floating somewhere far behind my eyes

[Chorus]
Breathe, just breathe, let it go
Let the river of the moment softly flow
The train will rise into the light, the fear will fade to gray
Just hold on through the darkness till it drifts away

[Verse]
The rumble turns to ocean and the lights dissolve
A gentle kind of falling that I can't resolve
My hands are made of static and my chest's a drum
But somewhere in the quiet I can hear the calm come

[Bridge]
In and out, slow and low, the storm will pass
Like a shadow on the water, like a breath on glass

[Chorus]
Breathe, just breathe, let it go
Let the river of the moment softly flow
The train will rise into the light, the fear will fade to gray
Just hold on through the darkness till it drifts away

subject: a card game that went too far
lyrics:
[Verse]
Under the mirror ball the cards came out to play
We were laughing in the sequins at the break of day
But the chips kept stacking higher and the wine ran red
And the game we played for fun got in our heads

[Chorus]
We went too far, too far tonight
Under the spinning silver light
The music's still playing but the love is gone
We gambled it all and we danced till dawn

[Verse]
Your diamond eyes were burning when you laid the queen
The coldest hand of poker that I've ever seen
The bassline kept on thumping while the table turned
And everything we promised was a bridge we burned

[Bridge]
Spin me round, spin me down, the deck is dealt
Never knew a winning hand could make you feel so felt

[Chorus]
We went too far, too far tonight
Under the spinning silver light
The music's still playing but the love is gone
We gambled it all and we danced till dawn

subject: looking back at your first open mic
lyrics:
[Verse]
The spotlight trembled on me as I stepped into the night
My fingers cold as winter and my heart a frightened light
The microphone a stranger and the crowd a darkened sea
The boy I was that evening is a ghost that follows me

[Chorus]
Oh the first time, oh the fire
When I dared to climb up higher
Every note a falling dancer, every word a sweet goodbye
To the trembling little dreamer 'neath the open evening sky

[Verse]
I remember how the silence held its breath before I sang
The way the room dissolved away the moment that I rang
A hundred nights have passed me by, a thousand stages more
But I'm still that frightened poet standing at the door

[Bridge]
Dance with me through memory where the young and brave begin
In the smoky little corner where I first let the music in

[Chorus]
Oh the first time, oh the fire
When I dared to climb up higher
Every note a falling dancer, every word a sweet goodbye
To the trembling little dreamer 'neath the open evening sky

subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
lyrics:
[Verse]
Stained glass watchin' as I walk out slow (slow)
Grew up in these pews but it's time to go (go)
Hymns in my head but they don't feel the same (nah)
Whisper a goodbye that don't nobody claim (shh)

[Chorus]
Quietly, quietly, slippin' out the door (out)
The faith that they gave me don't fit me no more (no more)
No big announcement, no scene, no fight (none)
Just a boy and his shadow walkin' into the night (night)

[Verse]
Mama still prayin' for my wanderin' soul (pray)
Pastor still preachin' 'bout the heaven and the coal (preach)
I loved every Sunday, every gospel song (love)
But somewhere down the line it started feelin' wrong (wrong)

[Bridge]
I ain't mad at God, I just gotta find my own (own)
Walk a different road, see the world on my own (lone)

[Chorus]
Quietly, quietly, slippin' out the door (out)
The faith that they gave me don't fit me no more (no more)
No big announcement, no scene, no fight (none)
Just a boy and his shadow walkin' into the night (night)
